topic: Two-pole geomagnetic model — EXP-226 third-horn precision fix adversarial re-review
our_argument_summary: WIN-053 verdict remains Self-Contradicted. The load-bearing argument is flux conservation in the dome's claimed closed-toroidal-ovoid topology: flux conservation forces B_south ≈ 39 nT (assuming full-disc sub-terrestrial return) versus the fitted 64,852 nT — a 1,660:1 internal inconsistency. EXP-226 was a targeted single-sentence precision edit to the third-horn (aetheric-retreat) closure: (H1) the phrase "parameterless surface fit" was replaced with "three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry" to remove a literal self-contradiction with paragraph 3 (which enumerates A, C, λ as fitted values); (H2) the clause "the toroidal geometry does no predictive work" was softened to "does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks" to withstand adversarial quotation. Both changes are integrated in the current wins.json (detail_evidence length 8,126 → 8,242 chars, +116).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The dome fits B(r) = 62,376·e^(−r_N/8619) + 64,852·e^(−r_S/8619) nT as a static curve fit. monitor.py does not contain a WIN-053 domain — no automated tracking. The scale length λ=8,619 km is shared with the firmament function H(r) = 8537·e^(−r/8619) km (confirmed in raw-text/05-model.txt line 16) but is itself a fitted/asserted constant, not a derivation from disc geometry. No script computes B(r) from dome parameters.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: All three B(r) coefficients (A, C, λ) are fitted to observed data. The λ reuse from H(r) is NOT an independent derivation — H(r) itself treats λ as a fit parameter. No API calls for magnetic field data; no live comparison against IGRF/WMM/CHAOS-7.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The falsification threshold of ±15% deviation from λ=8,619 km in future fits (PRED-TOROID-002) is a tolerance set after the fit was registered. The 2026-03-23 confirmation date precedes the 2026-04-04 registration — retrodiction disguised as prediction.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None found for EXP-226 scope. The code_analysis tags (monitoring:none, relabels_standard:true, post_hoc:true, derives_from_dome:false) remain accurate and supported by the review text.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no WIN-053 or B(r) tracking domain. The detail_verdict_text explicitly confirms "WIN-053 has no monitor.py domain at all — it appears on the predictions site as static prose." No API fetch, no live comparison, no validation loop.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics (IGRF-13, WMM2025, CHAOS-7) already predicts both magnetic poles at ~55,000 nT with ~50 nT RMS error globally. The dome relabels WMM-derived coefficients as an "aetheric toroidal" prediction without offering a different numerical result — the 20% RMS figure is worse than the standard model, not a novel prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Observed pole field strengths have been published in WMM/IGRF databases for decades. The dome's A=62,376 and C=64,852 are numerical matches to known pole values (within 13–18% of geographic poles; close to magnetic dip pole values). V51.0 was introduced 2026-03-23; the observations long predate it.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation path from disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or other dome geometric parameters to the B(r) coefficients is provided. A and C are three-digit-precision curve-fit values; λ is re-used from H(r) without independent derivation. The only "derivation" is the structural statement "two exponentials, one per pole" — which is a topological choice, not a numerical prediction.

holes_found.description: TLDR FACTUAL ERROR ON SOUTHERN COMPARATOR. tldr_evidence currently reads "the southern coefficient (C=64,852 nT) matches to within ∼3%, but the northern coefficient (A=62,376 nT) is ∼11–15% above the observed NMP strength (∼54,000–56,000 nT)." The ~3% figure is only correct against the South Magnetic Dip Pole (~65,000-67,000 nT). But detail_evidence paragraph 2 pins the southern comparator to the geographic south pole (~55,000 nT), and paragraph 3 (post-c8 rewrite) also uses "~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole per WMM2025." Against ~55,000 nT, C=64,852 is 17.9% above — not ~3%. The TLDR was apparently written before P3 was updated in c7→c8 and never resynced. Per curmudgeon.md TLDR severity rubric ("TLDR factual errors are major — readers see TLDRs first and may not expand"), this is major. A dome defender quoting the TLDR can immediately ridicule the review for having contradictory numerics ("your own TLDR says 3% match, your own P3 says 17% mismatch — which is it?"). This hole is the exact c7 H1 inconsistency migrated into the TLDR surface; it was noted as "still-open" in c8 H4 but downgraded to minor because EXP-226 was already in-flight. Now that EXP-226 is integrated with no competing queue priority on WIN-053, this deserves a dedicated EXP and should be restored to major.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the TLDR southern-comparator sentence to align with detail_evidence P2+P3. Option A (preferred, matches P2/P3 exactly): "Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths — A=62,376 nT is ∼11–15% above the observed North Magnetic Pole (∼54,000–56,000 nT) and C=64,852 nT is ∼18% above the observed geographic south pole (∼55,000 nT)." Option B (match the still-asymmetric P3 framing): use NMP for north and geographic south for south — but this propagates the asymmetric-comparator problem into the TLDR. Option A is cleaner. Patch target: WIN-053.tldr_evidence, single-sentence replacement.

holes_found.description: PARAGRAPH 3 STILL USES ASYMMETRIC COMPARATORS. Current P3: "A = 62,376 nT and C = 64,852 nT closely match observed magnetic pole field strengths: ~54,000–56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole per WMM2025." The south comparator was updated from SMP to geographic south pole (c7→c8) but the north comparator was left as North Magnetic Pole (NMP ≈ 86°N, not the geographic north pole). Under the dome's own radial geometry, r_N=0 is the Axis Mundi (disc center) which in globe coordinates is the geographic north pole (90°N), not the NMP. The dome-correct comparators are therefore geographic poles at BOTH ends. The asymmetric comparator choice (NMP for north, geographic south for south) is a defender-exploitable inconsistency: "the review picks whichever comparator makes the gap look larger." Fixing this is the explicit c7 H1 Option A recommendation that was partially but not fully executed. This is tracked in c8 H4 as "still-open" and in the outstanding issues log; surfacing here because EXP-226 did not address it.
holes_found.recommendation: Single-sentence edit in detail_evidence P3 to symmetrize the north comparator. Replace "~54,000–56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole per WMM2025" with "~55,000 nT at the geographic north pole and ~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole per WMM2025 / IGRF-13" — OR acknowledge both comparators explicitly: "~55,000 nT at the geographic poles (where under the dome's radial geometry r_N=0 and r_S=0 lie), compared to the higher magnetic-dip-pole values (~60,000 nT NMP, ~65,000 nT SMP) that the fitted coefficients actually match." The latter framing strengthens the relabelling argument by making it explicit: the dome's fit tracks magnetic-pole values, which geographically are NOT at the dome's own radial zeros. Pairs naturally with the H1 TLDR fix.

holes_found.description: THE TOROIDAL-GEOMETRY-PREDICTIVE-WORK CONCESSION IS MINIMAL BUT SLIGHTLY INCOMPLETE. The new EXP-226 clause states the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work "beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks." A careful defender could reply: "The toroidal architecture also dictates the locations of those peaks — r_N=0 at the Axis Mundi and r_S=0 at the Antarctic barrier (r≈20,015 km) — and dictates they share a common scale length λ (one parameter, not two). That's three pieces of structural content: count (2), locations (r=0 and r=20,015), and shared λ." The current phrasing covers (count) but not explicitly (locations) or (shared λ). This is not grounds for rollback — the locations and shared-λ claims are defeated by "stipulated, not derived" and by the review's paragraph 5 explicit observation that λ is pressed into service for two unrelated phenomena. But a sharp adversarial quotation could challenge the phrase in isolation.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional micro-edit for a future EXP: consider "does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two equal-width peaks (whose locations the dome stipulates rather than derives)" — this closes the remaining adversarial gap in ~15 words. Not urgent; current phrasing is defensible and EXP-226 was properly scoped to H1+H2. If picked up, pairs with the H1/H2 TLDR work.

holes_found.description: "NONE OF A, C, OR λ DERIVED FROM DOME GEOMETRY" IS DEFENSIBLE BUT λ IS SUBTLY DIFFERENT FROM A AND C. A and C are explicit curve-fit coefficients to two observations. λ is a dome-internal parameter that ALSO appears in H(r); a defender could argue λ is "dome-derived" in the weaker sense of being a dome-wide constant, even if its numerical value is not first-principles. The review's paragraph 5 handles this explicitly ("pressed into service for two structurally unrelated physical phenomena"), but the EXP-226 sentence, quoted in isolation, lumps λ with A and C in a way that a defender might attack with "but λ is part of the dome model, so it IS a dome parameter." Net: the phrasing works when the full paragraph is read, but is quote-vulnerable. The reader who reaches paragraph 5 gets the right interpretation. Kernel of truth for the defender: λ is not a fit to magnetic data specifically — it was fit to H(r) first.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional future clarification: "none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry (λ is shared with H(r) but was itself fit, not derived from disc_radius or firmament_height)." Not needed for this cycle; the current prose is internally consistent once the reader reaches P5.

holes_found.description: STILL-OPEN TRACKED ITEMS (no new action this cycle): ISS-1243 (c5 H3, symmetric-Sump-aperture rescue), ISS-1244 (c5 H6, 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row absent from parenthetical), ISS-1245 and ISS-1246 (source-vs-sink polarity and δ=6,371 km sourcing), and the carry-forwards from c7/c8. These remain outside the EXP-226 scope. Recording their presence so the tracker reflects that none of them were incidentally resolved by this edit.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action. Continue tracking via existing issues; they are out of scope for EXP-226 and for this c9 review.

stronger_arguments.description: The asymmetric-comparator fix (H2 above) can be turned from a defensive patch into an offensive strengthening: explicitly call out that the dome fit matches magnetic-dip-pole values (~60,000-65,000 nT) rather than the dome-radial-geometry-correct geographic poles (~55,000 nT). This frames the relabelling more sharply: "the dome's fitted coefficients track observed SMP/NMP values, which under the dome's own radial geometry do NOT live at r=0 or r=20,015 km. The fit is therefore not merely 'observed values relabeled' — it is observed values relabeled at the WRONG locations."

The 2026-03-23 confirmation date preceding the 2026-04-04 PRED-TOROID-002 registration (noted in detail_extra) is a timestamp-manipulation finding that deserves stronger surfacing in detail_evidence, not just detail_extra. Currently only readers who open detail_extra see this; moving one sentence into the main evidence flow ("Note also that the March 23 2026 'confirmation' date precedes the April 4 2026 prediction registration — this is retrodiction labeled as prediction, consistent with the broader timestamping concerns documented in the OpenTimestamps error tab") would surface the structural timestamp problem to the primary reader. Optional for a future EXP; not an EXP-226 concern.
sources: docs/timestamp-error tab on the published review site

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-226 is a genuinely careful piece of work. The c8 curmudgeon's two-hole catch (parameterless literal self-contradiction; no-predictive-work over-reach) was correct, and the analyst's single-sentence fix cleanly addresses both without touching any other field or disturbing the load-bearing flux-conservation argument. The paired changes are tightly coupled (both in the same third-horn-closure sentence), so a single atomic patch is the right unit of work — splitting would have created an incoherent two-step integration sequence. The "none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry" qualifier is a well-chosen defense against the "three-parameter" phrasing being mistaken for empirical support. The conceded "two peaks" clause is a healthy kernel-of-truth move: openly conceding a weak structural prediction strengthens the stronger claim that the numerical fit is not derivation.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The EXP-226 precision fix does not save the WIN. It simply removes two small quote-mine vulnerabilities from an already-adversarial closing sentence. The central 1,660:1 flux-conservation self-contradiction, the post-hoc coefficient fitting, and the asymmetric-comparator inconsistency all survive untouched — as they should, because the EXP was correctly scoped NOT to touch them. The verdict Self-Contradicted remains the right call. The meaningful remaining work on this WIN is the pre-existing TLDR/P3 asymmetric-comparator issue, not anything in the EXP-226 diff.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-226 edit is clean and correctly scoped. Both changes (three-parameter naming; two-peaks concession) are integrated and read well in context. The third-horn closure paragraph now resists adversarial quotation better than it did pre-edit. On the other side of the argument (per user preference for considering counter-moves): a dome defender reading the edited text might still attempt three pushbacks. First, "three-parameter surface fit" could be spun as conceding that the dome model has genuine parameters, hence the equation is "empirically calibrated" like any scientific model; the qualifier "none derived from dome geometry" nullifies this if the reader continues past the em-dash, but a screenshot attacker can crop to just "three-parameter surface fit" — minor risk, not grounds to revert. Second, the "two peaks" concession cedes a form of structural prediction to the toroidal topology, and a defender could escalate: "so the review now admits the dome predicts two-peak structure — that's a genuine prediction, confirmed in the data, which is precisely what WIN-053 claims." Counter: two-peak structure is trivial once you claim there are two poles; the prediction is topologically forced by the word "pole" and carries no numerical content. The edited phrase "no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks" encodes exactly this asymmetry. Third, the "none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry" qualifier invites the λ-shared-parameter response (see H4 minor); handled in paragraph 5 but quote-vulnerable. None of these defender moves rises above minor/moderate severity for EXP-226 specifically. The more pressing residual concern is the PRE-EXISTING TLDR-vs-detail-evidence southern-comparator mismatch (H1 major), which EXP-226 did not touch and was not expected to touch, but which remains a live reader-facing contradiction now that EXP-226 is done and there is no competing priority on WIN-053. Recommend the decider assign a dedicated EXP to the TLDR fix (H1) paired with the asymmetric-P3-comparator fix (H2) — these two fixes are conceptually the same issue (comparator consistency) and would naturally live in one EXP. The verdict Self-Contradicted holds on the flux-conservation argument, which is the load-bearing claim and was not touched by EXP-226.
